creator: satosi.kimura@nifty.ne.jp
text:
This patch is still essential on FreeBSD/i386 and gcc 4.2 to 4.9 
with -m128bit-long-double option.
 
 By the way, I have no information about long double on Clang.
So, I can say nothing about the combination of ports "ldouble" and
long double on this environment.
 
 I can say nothing whether this PR is still relevant or not with 
clear reason. At least I feel this PR becomes needless by the fact
as follows:

1. Above patch does not commited, but noboby complains.
2. __float128 and its libraries are available on gcc 4.6 or later.

 If you want to close this PR with rational reason, I will agree.
